questions.question: Problem-focused coping is generally most effective when:

options.text: The stressor is controllable and concrete steps can be taken
The person has limited social support

questions.explanation: Problem-focused coping involves taking direct action to change or eliminate the source of stress. It is most effective when the stressor is controllable — when there are concrete steps that can be taken to address the situation. For uncontrollable stressors, emotion-focused coping may be more appropriate.

questions.question: Research by Langer and Rodin with nursing home residents demonstrated that:

options.text: Problem-focused coping is always superior to emotion-focused coping
Exercise is the most effective stress reduction technique
Perceived control over daily decisions improved health and reduced mortality
Social support is more important than coping strategies

questions.explanation: Langer and Rodin found that nursing home residents given greater control over simple daily decisions showed improved health, greater happiness, and even lower mortality rates compared to those who had decisions made for them, demonstrating the powerful role of perceived control in stress management.
